failingTest: chunkText drops consecutive punctuation characters — existing guarantee chunks.join('') === trimmedOriginal is violated
fileMap: apps/web/src/lib/chunk-text.ts — modify — fix sentence-segmentation regex to capture consecutive delimiter runs without dropping characters
apps/web/src/lib/chunk-text.test.ts — modify — add regression tests for ellipses, ?!, . . ., hard-split interaction, and property-based join check
fixApproach: Modify the sentence-boundary regex in `chunkText` so that (a) the trailing delimiter capture uses `[.!?]+` (one or more) instead of `[.!?]` (exactly one), and (b) segments consisting solely of delimiters/spaces are not skipped. The core change is in the regex that tokenizes the trimmed input into sentence segments — currently it requires each segment to start with a non-delimiter and only captures one trailing delimiter, so any additional consecutive delimiters (as in `...`, `?!`, or `. . .`) are silently discarded. After the fix, every input character is assigned to exactly one segment, and the existing chunk-packing logic (which already respects maxLength and preserves inter-sentence whitespace on the front of the next chunk) naturally yields correct chunks whose join equals the trimmed input.
steps: Read the current implementation of `apps/web/src/lib/chunk-text.ts` to understand the exact sentence-segmentation regex and chunk-packing loop.
Read the existing test file (likely `apps/web/src/lib/chunk-text.test.ts` or co-located) to understand test structure and existing coverage.

Modify the sentence-segmentation regex to capture consecutive delimiter runs (`[.!?]+`) and to not require segments to begin with a non-delimiter character — ensuring every character of the trimmed input is assigned to a segment.
Verify that the chunk-packing loop still works correctly with the modified segments — no changes needed to packing logic if segments are correct.
Add regression test cases for the three reproductions from the issue: `chunkText('Wait... what?', 8)`, `chunkText('Really?! No way.', 9)`, `chunkText('End. . . done.', 6)`.
Add at least one property-based test that asserts `chunks.join('') === input.trim()` for a range of punctuation-heavy inputs.
Add at least one hard-split interaction test where a punctuation run straddles a maxLength boundary.
Run `npm run build:web` to confirm no type/build errors.
Run `npm run test` to confirm all tests pass (including new regression tests).
summary: Fix chunkText sentence-segmentation regex to capture consecutive punctuation runs (..., ?!, !!!, . . .) so no characters are dropped, restoring the exact-reconstruction guarantee.
verificationCommand: npm run build:web && npm run test
